visible_observations: A tall mature woman with blonde hair in a loose high bun stands squarely, holding a large closed book against one hip and extending her other hand toward the viewer.
She wears a fitted dark brown sleeveless long coat over trousers and boots; small script-like marks cover her neck and arms, and glossy black fluid coats the extended fingers and drips downward.
The book, inked hand, direct eye contact, and tailored vertical silhouette establish a controlled occult-scholar presentation.
defects: The upright stance, held book, fitted dark clothing, and extended spell hand closely resemble a familiar dark-academia caster template rather than exposing an unusual combat method.
Most of the visible script reads as decorative skin patterning, while the black fingertip drips are too localized to make a substantial bodily consequence legible.
The dark coat, trousers, boots, book, and background share compressed brown-black values, reducing separation through the lower body.
The calm display pose does not show a loaded attack, defensive response, or physical struggle with the apparent ink effect.
uncertainties: The image alone does not establish whether the script and black fluid are power, injury, corruption, or ordinary ornament.
The large book suggests a role but does not reveal whether it functions as weapon, shield, source, or carried reference.

visible_observations: Three candidates show a seated reader behind a very large open book, a standing woman holding a tall rectangular book while raising one hand, and a standing woman spreading a wide segmented document between outstretched arms.
The candidates vary pose and the book or document deployment while retaining a slim adult body, tailored dark clothing, and high-bun head shape.
defects: The exploration changes prop configuration more than body plan, garment family, or combat silhouette.
In the seated candidate, the open book and chair mass absorb the torso and legs; in the wide-document candidate, the rectangular sheet overwhelms the body.
The standing center candidate has the clearest body but retains a conventional upright caster outline with no strong directional action.
uncertainties: The A, B, and C labels do not explicitly state which candidate was selected.
The partly rendered presentation makes it difficult to distinguish pure silhouette decisions from interior costume and prop detail.

visible_observations: The face looks directly toward the viewer with slightly lowered lids, shaped brows, a level head, closed relaxed lips, and an otherwise controlled expression.
Loose blonde strands frame a conventionally attractive adult face; script-like markings rise from the chest up the neck and continue onto the visible arm.
defects: The polished skin, symmetrical beauty lighting, relaxed mouth, and mild narrowed gaze read primarily as a composed beauty portrait.
There is little visible jaw loading, mouth tension, cheek asymmetry, or head-angle conflict to distinguish manipulation, fear, restraint, hunger, or another specific state.
The black fluid on the hand is mostly outside the crop, leaving the face visually detached from its clearest unusual effect.
uncertainties: Unaided viewing cannot determine what she wants from the viewer, what she fears, or whether the direct gaze is predatory, skeptical, or merely self-possessed.
The script-like marks could be scars, writing, tattoos, or magic, and the crop provides no visible reaction to them.

visible_observations: At native size, the blonde head, raised arm, narrow dark body, split stance, long coat, and rectangular book remain visible.
The extended arm and book create the principal asymmetry against the dark background.
defects: The face, script markings, black fluid, tailoring, and book-surface design collapse at this size.
Dark trousers, coat tails, boots, and background merge through much of the lower half.
The surviving outline reads as a generic slim spellcaster holding a book, with little of the ink or inscription identity retained.
uncertainties: No neighboring unit icons, gameplay background, selection frame, or motion state are shown, so in-context recognition cannot be determined.
